tluaAndOrNotReserved.tlua(39,18): error TS1005: ';' expected.
tluaAndOrNotReserved.tlua(39,21): error TS1109: Expression expected.
tluaAndOrNotReserved.tlua(40,11): error TS1110: Type expected.
tluaAndOrNotReserved.tlua(40,15): error TS2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.


==== tluaAndOrNotReserved.tlua (33 errors) ====
    // `and`, `or` and `not` are keywords, so they are reserved. Every use as a name
    // falls into stock recovery, as with the class, decorator, array and this
    // removals. Lua reserves them too, so `t.and` is invalid there as well.
    
    local obj = { x = 1 };
    
    // Member names.
    local a1 = obj.and;
                   ~~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
                      ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local a2 = obj.or;
                   ~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
                     ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local a3 = obj.not;
                   ~~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
                      ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    
    // Property names in an object literal.
    local o1 = { and = 1 };
                 ~~~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
                     ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local o2 = { or = 1 };
                 ~~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
                    ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local o3 = { not = 1 };
                     ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    
    // Binding names.
    local and = 1;
          ~~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
              ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local or = 2;
          ~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
             ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local not = 3;
          ~~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
              ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    
    // Declaration names.
    function or() {}
            
!!! error TS7010: '(Missing)', which lacks return-type annotation, implicitly has an 'any' return type.
             ~~
!!! error TS1003: Identifier expected.
                ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
                  ~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
    
    // `not` shares KindExclamationToken with `!`, but only the punctuation spelling
    // is the non-null and definite-assignment operator. Accepting the word in those
    // roles would silently suppress a nil check.
    declare maybe: number | nil;
    local n1 = maybe not;
                     ~~~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
                        ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    local n2 not: number;
             ~~~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
                ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
                  ~~~~~~
!!! error TS2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
    // The punctuation spelling keeps both roles.
    local n3 = maybe!;
    local n4!: number;
            ~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
             ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
               ~~~~~~
!!! error TS2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
    // And the word keeps its own: prefix negation.
    local n5 = not maybe;
    
    // The type-position `!` (the JSDoc non-nullable operator) is punctuation-only
    // as well: the word `not` never silently becomes a type operator.
    local n6: number not;
                     ~~~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
                        ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    type N7 = not number;
              ~~~
!!! error TS1110: Type expected.
                  ~~~~~~
!!! error TS2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
